=== ./TestProject.csproj ===
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Exclude the nested xUnit test project so the web app build does not compile test sources. -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
    <None Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- SPA build integration.

       The frontend lives in /src (*.ts) and is compiled to /wwwroot/dist by the
       `npm run build` script (tsc + scripts/copy-assets.mjs). Since wwwroot/dist is
       gitignored, it must be (re)generated before the C# build so the static files
       are present for `UseStaticFiles`/publish. These targets wire npm into the
       MSBuild pipeline so `dotnet build` and `dotnet publish` are self-contained.

       NpmInstall is incremental: it only runs when package.json or package-lock.json
       change (or node_modules is absent). node_modules/.package-lock.json is npm's
       own stamp file and serves as the output/up-to-date marker. -->
  <Target Name="NpmInstall"
          Inputs="package.json;package-lock.json"
          Outputs="node_modules/.package-lock.json">
    <Exec Command="npm install" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

  <!-- NpmBuild always runs (tsc is fast and honors noEmitOnError), but only after
       deps are in place. Runs before BeforeBuild so dist exists prior to CoreBuild
       and static-asset copy during publish. -->
  <Target Name="NpmBuild"
          DependsOnTargets="NpmInstall"
          BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild">
    <Exec Command="npm run build" WorkingDirectory="$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)" />
  </Target>

</Project>

content.text:
Based on my review, the implementation is complete and correct.

**Completion against task:** The `NormalizeRelativePath` private static helper was added and mirrors the frontend's `normalizeRelativePath` (src/format.ts) rules exactly: replace `\` with `/`, split on `/`, filter out empty/`.`/`..` segments, join with `/`, and return empty string for null/empty. It is applied only to the response path.

**Preserved behavior:** The `_service.UploadAsync(path ?? string.Empty, file)` call still receives the **raw** query value (verified by `Upload_PassesRawPathToService_EvenWhenNonCanonical`). Status codes and try/catch structure are unchanged. Already-canonical inputs produce identical output (verified by `Upload_ResponsePath_IsAlreadyCanonical_ForCleanInputs`).

**Compliance/cleanliness:** Follows existing controller conventions (XML doc comments on the helper, `private static`, matches the catch-block style). `System.Linq` is available via `ImplicitUsings=enable`. No dead code, no magic literals, no scope creep.

**Verification:** `dotnet test` reports 227 passed / 0 failed, including the newly added normalization tests covering backslashes, leading/trailing slashes, repeated slashes, dot/dotdot segments, collapse-to-empty, and a combined mixed-input case.

It is applied only to the response path while the `_service.UploadAsync` call continues to receive the raw query value, so upload success/failure status codes and the service interaction are unchanged. Behavior is identical for already-canonical inputs. The helper is documented with an XML doc comment and integrates cleanly with the existing controller style (`System.Linq` is available via ImplicitUsings). Comprehensive tests cover backslashes, leading/trailing slashes, repeated slashes, dot/dotdot segments, collapse-to-empty, and mixed input, plus a regression test confirming the raw path is forwarded to the service. Full suite: 227 passed, 0 failed. No completion, compliance, dead-code, readability, or behavior-preservation issues found.",
